title: cistus laurifolius subsp. laurifolius
intro: Cistus laurifolius subsp. laurifolius, also known as Laurel-leaved Rockrose, is a shrub species in the Cistus genus, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves that resemble those of the bay tree. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cistus laurifolius subsp. laurifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
